top_signals.title: SLB, Liberty Energy to Sell Infrastructure to Data Centers
top_signals.summary: SLB, the world’s largest oil field service provider, has agreed to partner with Liberty Energy Inc. to provide modular systems and power for data centers, the latest example of energy companies positioning themselves to benefit from the artificial intelligence boom.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: 9 nations back Ukraine’s Patriot alternative, Freyja — and want it flying in a year
top_signals.summary: Ballistic missiles are the one threat Ukraine can't stop with a weapon it makes itself.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: New CrashStealer malware poses as Apple crash reporting tool
top_signals.summary: A new macOS information-stealing malware called CrashStealer pretends to be Apple's crash-reporting tool to steal credentials, keychain data, and crypto wallets. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX alumni building remote-controlled construction equipment land $115 million fund round
top_signals.summary: TerraFirma on Tuesday said it raised $115 to support new hires and the building of a new factory and mission control center.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Citi Stock Traders Post Record Revenue for Quarter
top_signals.summary: Citigroup says revenue from equities trading surged 45% to $2.3 billion in the second quarter from a year earlier. That's 11% higher than the record set in this year's opening months. Earnings per share came in at $3.15, beating all estimates. This is coverage from "Bloomberg Surveillance." (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Wetherspoon and Young’s toast World Cup success as shares rocket
top_signals.summary: Wetherspoon and other top London-listed pub groups have surged in value over the course of the World Cup as millions of revellers toasted the hot weather and the Three Lions’ success. Tim Martin’s budget pub chain, which is listed on the FTSE 250, has gained more than £75m in value since the start of the [...]
